title: 4 Files Incoming to Central 1
body_text:
4	Files Incoming to Central 1
4.1	Overview of Files Incoming to Central 1
Incoming files are created by financial institutions or banking system providers and uploaded to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange for further processing (e.g., settlement).
4.2	Central 1âs AFT File Specifications
This document provides the specifications for AFT transactions that originators submit to Central 1 via file transmission. Originators that upload AFT files to Central 1 must ensure that their files comply with Central 1âs AFT file specifications. These specifications are based on CPA Standard 005, Standards for the Exchange of Financial Data on AFT Files, with some modifications. For details on file structure and format, see Central 1âs AFT File Specifications,
Form 2726
.
4.3	AgriInvest Activity File Specifications
The purpose of this document is to report AgriInvest file activity. Financial institutions can use the file specifications provided in this document as
the file specifications for the Comma Separated Value (CSV) file, which is the required format for file submission to Central 1; or
the field definitions for the Excel template provided by Central 1 to assist financial institutions in capturing the required reporting data and generating the CSV file.
For details on file structure and format, see AgriInvest Activity File Specifications,
Form 3794
.
4.4	I & J Batch Returns File Specifications (BC Only)
The I & J Batch Returns file contains direct deposit returns or pre-authorized debit returns information that financial institutions send to Central 1 via the File and Report Exchange. Central 1 then forwards the transactions to the originating Direct Clearers for processing. For details on file structure and format, see I & J Batch Returns File Specifications (BC Only),
Form 6273
.
4.5	Bill Payment Batch File Specifications
The Bill Payment batch file is used by financial institutions that arenât enrolled in Central 1âs online Bill Payment System. For details on file structure and format, see Bill Payment Batch File Specifications,
Form 6276
.
4.6	Cheque Return File Specifications
The Cheque Return File is used by financial institutions that want to send a file with cheques to be returned instead of manually entering the returns in Central 1âs Online Return System (ORS). For details on file structure and format, see Cheque Return File Specifications,
Form 6280
.
4.7	AFT Return File Specifications
The AFT Return file contains AFT items provided by your financial institution that are to be dishonoured and returned. Files must be created and submitted separately for CAD and USD items.
Your financial institution can enter individual items via the Online Return System (ORS) Web front-end. Alternatively, you can upload batches of AFT returns to ORS instead of entering individual items via the ORS Web front-end. You can use your banking system or an in-house system to create AFT Return files to be uploaded to ORS. See the Online Return System Part for more information.
For details on file structure and format, see AFT Return File Specifications,
Form 9335
.
4.8	Notice of Change (NOC) File Specifications
The AFT Notice of Change (NOC) file contains a listing of NOC items that Central 1 sends to the Originating Direct Clearer electronically. Files must be created and submitted separately for CAD and USD items.
Your financial institution can enter individual items via the Online Return System (ORS) Web front-end. Alternatively, you can upload batches of AFT NOC items to ORS instead of entering individual items via the ORS Web front-end. You can use your banking system or an in-house system to create AFT NOC files to be uploaded to ORS. See the Online Return System Part for more information.
For details on file structure and format, see Notice of Change (NOC) File Specifications,
Form 9336
.
END
